response:
To compute the **sample standard deviation** of the data set:

$$
\{-11, 9, 5, 15, -10, -15, 12, -25, -22\}
$$

---

### Step 1: Find the mean ($\bar{x}$)

$$
\bar{x} = \frac{-11 + 9 + 5 + 15 - 10 - 15 + 12 - 25 - 22}{9}
$$

$$
\bar{x} = \frac{(-11 - 10 - 15 - 25 - 22) + (9 + 5 + 15 + 12)}{9}
$$

$$
\bar{x} = \frac{(-63) + (41)}{9} = \frac{-22}{9} \approx -2.4444
$$

---

### Step 2: Compute the squared differences from the mean

$$
\begin{align*}
(-11 - \bar{x})^2 &= (-11 + \frac{22}{9})^2 = \left(-\frac{77}{9}\right)^2 = \frac{5929}{81} \\
9 - \bar{x} &= \left(9 + \frac{22}{9}\right) = \frac{107}{9} \Rightarrow \left(\frac{107}{9}\right)^2 = \frac{11449}{81} \\
5 - \bar{x} &= \left(5 + \frac{22}{9}\right) = \frac{67}{9} \Rightarrow \left(\frac{67}{9}\right)^2 = \frac{4489}{81} \\
15 - \bar{x} &= \left(15 + \frac{22}{9}\right) = \frac{157}{9} \Rightarrow \left(\frac{157}{9}\right)^2 = \frac{24649}{81} \\
-10 - \bar{x} &= \left(-10 + \frac{22}{9}\right) = -\frac{78}{9} = -\frac{26}{3
